title: A New Complexity Theory for the Quantum Age | Quanta Magazine
why:
- Traditional complexity theory cannot accommodate problems with quantum inputs and outputs, addressing only classical-to-classical transformations.
- Henry Yuen is leading an effort to develop a new "fully quantum" complexity theory to handle problems where both inputs and outputs are inherently quantum.
- Quantum cryptographic concepts like bit commitment behave fundamentally differently when quantum inputs are involved, revealing new complexity-theoretic challenges.
- Recent research by Yuen and collaborators has identified that many seemingly unrelated quantum problems are actually equivalent, suggesting deep underlying structure in this new theory.
- The logical relationship between classical and fully quantum complexity theories remains an open and fundamental question.
